Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Calculate the greatest common divisor (GCD) of two non-negative integers using the iterative Euclidean algorithm.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module Gcd
  (defun gcd (a b)
    (while (> b 0)
      (def temp b)
      (set b (math_mod a b))
      (set a temp))
    a)

  (test standard     (eq? (gcd 48 18) 6))
  (test multiple     (eq? (gcd 100 25) 25))
  (test coprime      (eq? (gcd 17 23) 1))
  (test first_zero   (eq? (gcd 0 42) 42))
  (test second_zero  (eq? (gcd 42 0) 42))
  (test both_zero    (eq? (gcd 0 0) 0))
  (test large_nums   (eq? (gcd 1701 3768) 3)))

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Subtract constant from array: write a small AGC module.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module ArraySubtractConstant
  (defun subtract_constant_from_array (arr constant)
    (def len (arr_length arr))
    (def result_arr (arr_new len))
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i len)
      (def original_val (arr_get arr i))
      (def new_val (- original_val constant))
      (arr_set result_arr i new_val)
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    result_arr)

  (test subtract_constant_from_empty_array
    (eq? (subtract_constant_from_array (arr_new 0) 5) (arr_new 0)))

  (test subtract_constant_from_positive_numbers
    (eq? (subtract_constant_from_array (arr_new 3 10 20 30) 5) (arr_new 3 5 15 25)))

  (test subtract_constant_from_negative_numbers
    (eq? (subtract_constant_from_array (arr_new 2 1 -2 -3) 2) (arr_new 2 -1 -3 -5)))

  (test subtract_constant_from_mixed_numbers
    (eq? (subtract_constant_from_array (arr_new 3 10 0 -20 50 40) 5) (arr_new 3 6 15 -30 55 35)))

  (test subtract_constant_from_single_element
    (eq? (subtract_constant_from_array (arr_new 1 7) 3) (arr_new 1 4)))

  (test subtract_constant_from_duplicates
    (eq? (subtract_constant_from_array (arr_new 2 10 10 50) 5) (arr_new 2 5 5 25)))
)

Count zeros in array: write a small AGC module.

(module ZerosInArray
  (defun count_zeros (xs)
    (def count 0)
    (def i 0)
    (def n (arr_length xs))
    (while (< i n)
      (def current_val (arr_get xs i))
      (if (= current_val 0)
        (set count (+ count 1)))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    count)

  (defun make_array (a b c d e)
    (def xs (arr_new 5))
    (arr_set xs 0 a)
    (arr_set xs 1 b)
    (arr_set xs 2 c)
    (arr_set xs 3 d)
    (arr_set xs 4 e)
    xs)

  (test empty_array
    (eq? (count_zeros (arr_new 0)) 0))

  (test no_zeros
    (eq? (count_zeros (make_array 1 2 3 4 5)) 0))

  (test some_zeros
    (eq? (count_zeros (make_array 1 0 3 0 5)) 2))

  (test all_zeros
    (eq? (count_zeros (make_array 0 0 0 0 0)) 5))

  (test single_zero
    (eq? (count_zeros (make_array 0 1 2 3 4)) 1))

  (test with_negative_numbers
    (eq? (count_zeros (make_array -1 0 -2 0 3)) 2))
)

(module TipSplit

    (/ (math_ceil (* x 100)) 100))
  (defun per_share (subtotal tip_pct people)
    (def tip (* subtotal (/ tip_pct 100)))
    (def total (+ subtotal tip))
    (ceil_cents (/ total people)))

  (test odd (near? (per_share 53.33 15 3) 20.44 0.01))

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Write a function that reads the content of a file and returns only its first line. The first line is defined as all text up to, but not including, the first newline character (`\n`). If the file does not contain a newline, the entire content is considered the first line.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module ReadFirstLine
  (extern defun read_file (path) @capability "file.read")

  (defun get_first_line (path)
    (def content (read_file path))
    (def newline_pos (str_index_of content "\n"))
    (if (< newline_pos 0)
      content
      (str_substring content 0 newline_pos)))

  (test multi_line
    (mocks (file.read "data.txt" "hello world\ngoodbye world"))
    (eq? (get_first_line "data.txt") "hello world"))

  (test single_line_no_newline
    (mocks (file.read "single.txt" "a single line"))
    (eq? (get_first_line "single.txt") "a single line"))

  (test single_line_with_newline
    (mocks (file.read "single_nl.txt" "just one\n"))
    (eq? (get_first_line "single_nl.txt") "just one"))

  (test empty_file
    (mocks (file.read "empty.txt" ""))
    (eq? (get_first_line "empty.txt") ""))

  (test starts_with_newline
    (mocks (file.read "leading_nl.txt" "\nsecond line"))
    (eq? (get_first_line "leading_nl.txt") ""))

  (test only_newline
    (mocks (file.read "only_nl.txt" "\n"))
    (eq? (get_first_line "only_nl.txt") ""))
)

Recipe scale by servings: write a small AGC module.

(module ScaledRecipe
  (defun scale_recipe_quantity (original_quantity scale_factor)
    (* original_quantity scale_factor))

  (defun scale_recipe (original_ingredients scale_factor)
    (def scaled_ingredients (arr_new 5))
    (def i 0)
    (def n (arr_length original_ingredients))
    (while (< i n)
      (arr_set scaled_ingredients i (scale_recipe_quantity (arr_get original_ingredients i) scale_factor))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    scaled_ingredients)

  (test simple_scale
    (def ingredients (arr_new 2))
    (arr_set ingredients 0 100)
    (arr_set ingredients 1 150)
    (def new_ingredients (scale_recipe ingredients 2.5))
    (eq? (arr_get new_ingredients 0) 250.0)
    (eq? (arr_get new_ingredients 1) 375.0))

  (test complex_scale
    (def ingredients (arr_new 4))
    (arr_set ingredients 0 0.5)
    (arr_set ingredients 1 2.0)
    (arr_set ingredients 2 5.0)
    (arr_set ingredients 3 10.0)
    (def new_ingredients (scale_recipe ingredients 0.75))
    (near? (arr_get new_ingredients 0) 0.375 0.01)
    (near? (arr_get new_ingredients 1) 1.5 0.01)
    (near? (arr_get new_ingredients 2) 3.75 0.01)
    (near? (arr_get new_ingredients 3) 7.5 0.01))

  (test zero_scale
    (def ingredients (arr_new 1))
    (arr_set ingredients 0 100)
    (def new_ingredients (scale_recipe ingredients 0.0))
    (eq? (arr_get new_ingredients 0) 0.0))
    (eq? (arr_get new_ingredients 1) 0.0))

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Sum the decimal digits of n.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module SumDigits
  (defun sum_digits (n)
    (def s 0)
    (def cur n)
    (while (> cur 0)
      (set s (+ s (math_mod cur 10)))
      (set cur (math_floor (/ cur 10))))
    s)
  (test zero (eq? (sum_digits 0) 0))
  (test single (eq? (sum_digits 7) 7))
  (test multi (eq? (sum_digits 1234) 10))
  (test big (eq? (sum_digits 99999) 45)))

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Write a function that counts the number of words in a string. Words are separated by one or more spaces, and the function should correctly handle leading, trailing, and multiple consecutive spaces.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module WordCount
  (defun count_words (s)
    (def parts (str_split s " "))
    (def count 0)
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i (arr_length parts))
      (def part (arr_get parts i))
      (if (> (str_length part) 0)
        (set count (+ count 1)))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    count)

  (test empty_string (eq? (count_words "") 0))
  (test single_word (eq? (count_words "hello") 1))
  (test two_words (eq? (count_words "hello world") 2))
  (test extra_spaces (eq? (count_words " leading  and   trailing ") 3))
  (test all_spaces (eq? (count_words "    ") 0))
  (test complex (eq? (count_words " a b  c   d    e ") 5)))

Http get body's first word: write a small AGC module.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module FirstWordBody
  (extern defun http_get (url) @capability "http.fetch")

  (defun get_first_word (url)
    (def body (http_get url))
    (def trimmed_body (str_trim body)) ; Trim spaces at start and end
    (if (str_eq trimmed_body "")
      (return ""))
    (def words (str_split trimmed_body " "))
    ; An empty string is an empty array element. Or, str_split "" " " is [""].
    ; We use str_split " " "" to avoid str_split "" " " leading to [""].
    (arr_get words 0))

  (test simple_word
    (mocks (http.fetch "https://example.com/hello" "Hello World! This is a test."))
    (eq? (get_first_word "https://example.com/hello") "Hello"))

  (test no_space_after_word
    (mocks (http.fetch "https://example.com/world" "One Two Three"))
    (eq? (get_first_word "https://example.com/world") "One"))

  (test single_word_body
    (mocks (http.fetch "https://example.com/single" "OnlyWord"))
    (eq? (get_first_word "https://example.com/single") "OnlyWord"))

  (test empty_body
    (mocks (http.fetch "https://example.com/empty" ""))
    (eq? (get_first_word "https://example.com/empty") ""))

  (test body_with_multiple_spaces
    (mocks (http.fetch "https://example.com/multi" "  first   word  "))
    (eq? (get_first_word "https://example.com/multi") "first"))

  (test body_with_numbers_and_symbols
    (mocks (http.fetch "https://example.com/token" "123!@#456$%"))
    (eq? (get_first_word "https://example.com/token") "123!@#456$%")))

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Create a function to classify a person's heart rate into one of five training zones based on their age and current heart rate. The classification relies on calculating the maximum heart rate (MHR) and then determining which percentage-based zone the current heart rate falls into.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module HeartRateZones
  (defun calculate_mhr (age)
    "Calculates the estimated maximum heart rate using the 220 - age formula."
    (- 220 age))

  (defun get_heart_rate_zone (age current_hr)
    "Determines the heart rate zone based on age and current heart rate."
    (def mhr (calculate_mhr age))
    (if (< current_hr (* mhr 0.5)) (return "Below Zone 1"))
    (if (< current_hr (* mhr 0.6)) (return "Zone 1"))
    (if (< current_hr (* mhr 0.7)) (return "Zone 2"))
    (if (< current_hr (* mhr 0.8)) (return "Zone 3"))
    (if (< current_hr (* mhr 0.9)) (return "Zone 4"))
    (if (<= current_hr mhr) (return "Zone 5"))
    "Above Maximum")

  (test below_zone (eq? (get_heart_rate_zone 30 94) "Below Zone 1"))
  (test zone_1 (eq? (get_heart_rate_zone 40 100) "Zone 1"))
  (test zone_2 (eq? (get_heart_rate_zone 50 115) "Zone 2"))
  (test zone_3 (eq? (get_heart_rate_zone 25 150) "Zone 3"))
  (test zone_4 (eq? (get_heart_rate_zone 35 160) "Zone 4"))
  (test zone_5_edge (eq? (get_heart_rate_zone 60 160) "Zone 5"))
  (test above_max (eq? (get_heart_rate_zone 20 201) "Above Maximum")))

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Write a function that reads a file's content and counts the number of lines. This function should correctly handle empty files, files with or without a trailing newline, and files containing blank lines.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module FileLineCounter
  (extern defun read_file_content (path) @capability "file.read")

  (defun count_lines (path)
    (def content (read_file_content path))
    (if (str_eq content "")
      (return 0))

    (def parts (str_split content "\n"))
    (def num_parts (arr_length parts))

    (def last_part (arr_get parts (- num_parts 1)))
    (if (str_eq last_part "")
      (- num_parts 1)
      num_parts))

  (test empty_file
    (mocks (file.read "empty.txt" ""))
    (eq? (count_lines "empty.txt") 0))

  (test single_line_no_newline
    (mocks (file.read "one.txt" "hello world"))
    (eq? (count_lines "one.txt") 1))

  (test single_line_with_newline
    (mocks (file.read "one_nl.txt" "hello world\n"))

    (* balance monthly_rate))

(module HttpStatusChecker
  (extern defun fetch_url (url) @capability "http.fetch")

  (defun get_status_message (url)
    (def response (fetch_url url))
    (def parts (str_split response ":"))
    (def code (arr_get parts 0))

    (if (str_eq code "200")
      (return "OK"))
    (if (str_eq code "404")

    (eq? (get_status_message "https://example.com/missing") "Not Found"))

  (test server_error_status
    (mocks (http.fetch "https://api.example.com/fail" "500:Internal Server Error"))
    (eq? (get_status_message "https://api.example.com/fail") "Server Error"))

  (test unhandled_redirect_status
    (mocks (http.fetch "http://old.site" "301:Moved Permanently"))
    (eq? (get_status_message "http://old.site") "Unknown Status: 301"))

  (test unhandled_client_error
    (mocks (http.fetch "https://example.com/bad-request" "400:Bad Request"))
    (eq? (get_status_message "https://example.com/bad-request") "Unknown Status: 400"))

(module SubtractConstantFromArray
  (defun subtract_constant_from_array (arr constant)
    (def len (arr_length arr))
    (def result_arr (arr_new len))
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i len)
      (def original_val (arr_get arr i))
      (def new_val (- original_val constant))
      (arr_set result_arr i new_val)
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    result_arr)

  (test basic_subtraction
    (def arr (arr_new 3))
    (arr_set arr 0 10)
    (arr_set arr 1 20)
    (arr_set arr 2 30)
    (def result (subtract_constant_from_array arr 5))
    (eq? (arr_get result 0) 5))
    (eq? (arr_get result 1) 15))

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module HeartRateZone
  (defun calculate_mhr (age)
    (- 220 age))

  (defun get_zone_percentage (current_hr mhr)
    (* (/ current_hr mhr) 100.0))

  (defun classify_zone (pct)
    (if (< pct 50) (return "Resting"))
    (if (< pct 60) (return "Zone 1: Very Light"))
    (if (< pct 70) (return "Zone 2: Light"))
    (if (< pct 80) (return "Zone 3: Moderate"))
    (if (< pct 90) (return "Zone 4: Hard"))
    "Zone 5: Maximum")

  (defun get_heart_rate_zone (age current_hr)
    (def mhr (calculate_mhr age))
    (if (<= mhr 0) (return "Invalid Age"))
    (def pct (get_zone_percentage current_hr mhr))
    (classify_zone pct))

  (test resting_zone
    (eq? (get_heart_rate_zone 30 90) "Resting"))

  (test zone_1_very_light
    (eq? (get_heart_rate_zone 25 110) "Zone 1: Very Light"))

  (test zone_2_light_boundary
    (eq? (get_heart_rate_zone 20 120) "Zone 2: Light"))

  (test zone_3_moderate
    (eq? (get_heart_rate_zone 40 135) "Zone 3: Moderate"))

  (test zone_4_hard
    (eq? (get_heart_rate_zone 35 160) "Zone 4: Hard"))

  (test zone_5_maximum
